description:
## The question

\`fz-5j5.2\` brought the codegen pipeline from 3 \`type_module\` calls
to 2. Under \`fz run\`, debug instrumentation showed type_module
firing 4 times for ast_eval — not 2. The extra 2 calls are
unaccounted for.

## Scope

Investigation only. No code change in this ticket; outcome is one
of:

  (a) Find a redundant call path → file a follow-up fix ticket.
  (b) Find a legitimate second consumer (e.g., \`spec_check.rs\`
      validating after compile; \`test_runner.rs\` re-typing for
      its parity matrix) → document why in a comment near the
      additional call site.

## How to investigate

\`\`\`
git grep -nE 'type_module|compile_pipeline|ir_codegen::compile' \\
  src/main.rs src/test_runner.rs src/repl.rs src/runtime.rs \\
  src/spec_check.rs src/ir_fold.rs src/ir_inline.rs
\`\`\`

Then add a stacktrace-on-entry temporarily to \`type_module\` and
run \`./target/release/fz run fixtures/ast_eval/input.fz\` once.
Identify the four callers, classify, document or file.

## Acceptance

Either a follow-up ticket exists for a redundant call, or a comment
near each call site explains why it's necessary. The count is no
longer mysterious.

## Independent of

All other PROV tickets.
title: fz-PROV.2: investigate why type_module is called 4x under fz run
